programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On a cold December night in 1993, Rose Larner stopped in a convenience store on her way to her boyfriend's house. She was never seen or heard from again. Rose's disappearance remained a mystery, until a tiny clue found years later revealed a tragic tale of drugs, romance and revenge.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A police investigation of a pediatrician, after the mysterious death of one of her patients, leads them to a Texas hospital where the pediatric mortality rate was higher than at any hospital in the country. The common link was nurse Genene Jones, but proving the connection seems impossible until an international team of doctors uncovers an unlikely murder weapon.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Hikers near Anchorage, Alaska discovered a body wrapped in sheets which were edged in orange stitching. Clinging to the sheet, they also discovered a tuft of red carpet fibers _ threads of evidence which led them straight to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1988, the body of a young woman was discovered in an Ohio river. Most of the evidence had been washed away, but the victim's 6 year-old son unwittingly told investigators all they needed to know...without saying a word. The clue was in his genes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Robert Sims returned home after working the night shift, and found his wife, Paula, unconscious on the kitchen floor. Their two-year-old son, Randy, was asleep in an upstairs bedroom, but their six-week-old daughter, Heather, was missing. Paula Sims was the only witness to a crime that baffles investigators to this very day.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The disappearance of a mail-order bride proves suspicious when detectives learn that two of the Texas husband's previous wives had died in mysterious circumstances.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The police investigate when a severed leg is discovered in the garbage. Conventional means of identifying the victim are impossible, leading investigators to rely on DNA, forensic anthropology and toxicology to identify the victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two men were murdered while sleeping in their beds. The next night, arson destroyed a family planning clinic. Investigators wonder whether some shards of glass, paint chips and a chicken feather could link what appeared to be two separate crimes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In a quiet village in Great Britain, a farmer came upon a chilling sight. Impaled on his fence post was a severed lamb's head along with a note which read, "You next." The author of the note didn't elaborate on why the farmer had been targeted, but between the lines, he'd said plenty.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police suspect Dr. John 'Jack' Boyle in the disappearance of his wife Noreen when he signs mortgage papers for a new house with a woman pretending to be his spouse.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1999, nine-year-old Valiree Jackson vanished on her way to school near Spokane, WA. Her father Brad Jackson reported the disappearance and the entire community started searching for her abductor.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Heavy rains and snow run-off carry manure into Milwaukee's water supply in 1993, leading to an epidemic of gastrointestinal illness affecting 400,000 people.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Using an anonymous letter and geographical profiling, investigators close in on a serial rapist.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On September 17, 1984, in a suburb of Tucson, Arizona, eight-year-old Vicki Hoskins left home on her pink bicycle to mail a letter for her mother. She never returned, but her slightly damaged bicycle was found nearby. Investigators turned to forensic science, in the hope it would tell them not only what happened to Vicki, but also who was responsible.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The news of a flight attendant's brutal murder prompted the son of a victim who had been killed five years earlier to contact police. Both murders had similar MOs, and analysis of biological evidence proved that these were serial killings.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In this classic episode of Forensic Files, the longest running true crime series in television history, a serial bomber was waging a vendetta against the legal profession: A judge, two attorneys and even a courthouse had been targeted. Employing a range of forensic techniques, investigators used evidence from the bombs to lead them to the perpetrator.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After a street fight took the life of a national wrestling champion, police had to determine if he was killed in cold blood, or in self-defense. A jury decided it was murder, and sentenced the accused to a minimum of twenty years in prison.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: It was supposed to be a routine motorcade for the Queen of England. But on the way to Yosemite National Park, a car carrying three Secret Service agents collided with a car driven by a deputy from the local sheriff's office.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Early one morning in a deserted area outside of Phoenix, a motorcyclist discovered the body of a young woman. She had been beaten, bound, strangled and possibly raped. The nearby plants would tell investigators more about the killer than any other single piece of evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: One investigator's quick thinking helps him capture a shoe impression made in snow before the evidence melts away. The shoe leads investigators to Peter Benedict and Frank Lanoue, who are convicted of aggravated assault and robbery.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An infant was rushed to a Cleveland emergency room with serious breathing problems. The baby's lungs were bleeding. Within months, there were more than 30 cases -- an incidence more than a thousand times higher than anywhere else in the world. Doctors had never seen anything like it, and searched frantically for the cause and a cure.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, 16-month-old Anne Grace Gimmenstad became very ill, was rushed to the hospital and died within two weeks. Following extensive medical examinations, it was determined that Anna had been the victim of the deadly bacteria E.coli.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1990, Shirley Andronowich's body was found one morning after being brutally murdered and mutilated in Winnipeg, Manitoba. Witnesses told police Shirley and her husband Ed were seen fighting in a bar the night before. Police first suspected Ed and even arrested him after he confessed to the murder. However, all forensic evidence proved that Ed had actually nothing to do with the murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When 180 Legionnaires contract pneumonia-like symptoms after a Philadelphia Convention and 29 of them die, doctors and scientists are mystified. The determination of one scientist helps to determine the cause and likely vector of this deadly disease.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: FBI psychological profiling and DNA evidence identify Timothy Wilson Spencer who raped and strangled five young women in Virginia. The U.S. criminal justice system's first use of DNA profiling in a serial murder case frees an innocent man after he spent two years in prison, and convicts a real killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two different men called police to report the same murder. Apparently, neither one knew that the other had called. The investigation uncovered even more unusual circumstances.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: With no forensic evidence inside a murder scene, investigators were baffled. But they suspected that the victim's dog had witnessed the crime. If she had, forensic scientists would have to find a way to find out what the dog had seen.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For seven years, a trio of men robbed one bank after another. They always got away before the police arrived, and they left no evidence behind. But the way they stood and the clothes they wore told a story that could be read by forensic scientists.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: This examination of the role an autopsy plays in a murder investigation focuses on the suspicious 1997 death of Georgia resident Virginia Ridley. Police charged her mentally unstable husband Alan with murder, but a medical examiner discovered that she died of natural causes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two different men called police to report the same murder. Apparently, neither one knew that the other had called. The investigation uncovered even more unusual circumstances.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: California police had several suspects in the robbery and brutal murder of an elderly couple. Ten years later, a determined forensic scientist used material from a dentist's office to uncover ballistic evidence which had been overlooked.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An examination of the 2002 DC area shooting spree that resulted in ten murders.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Shortly after daybreak in Vancouver, British Columbia, a fire was set in a dumpster. No one saw either the arsonist or the fire, and it burned for hours in the deserted parking lot. But there was more than garbage in the container, and it would take sophisticated science to find the evidence in the ashes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man died in his home after a long history of heart disease. It was assumed that this was the cause of his death. A few weeks after the funeral, rumors surfaced that the death was no accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A healthy young man dies mysteriously in his sleep, and toxicology tests reveal a lethal dose of lidocaine in his blood. Investigators find a suicide note, and presume he killed himself until a forensic linguist examines the note.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of a young woman was found under an isolated ramp of the Interstate, and a tiny, unique fiber on the victim's clothing led San Diego police straight to the most unlikely of killers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1990, Shirley Andronowich's body was found one morning after being brutally murdered and mutilated in Winnipeg, Manitoba. Witnesses told police Shirley and her husband Ed were seen fighting in a bar the night before. Police first suspected Ed and even arrested him after he confessed to the murder. However, all forensic evidence proved that Ed had actually nothing to do with the murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman identifies her husband as her attacker, but questions arise 16 years later regarding her recollections.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1993, the Amtrak Railroad experienced the deadliest train crash in United States history when the Sunset Limited derailed while crossing Alabama's Big Bayou Canot bridge. Forty-seven passengers and crew were killed; scores more were injured.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Betty Wolsieffer, the wife of a well-known dentist Glen Wolsieffer is found dead, police are unsure who killed her and injured her husband. Some fibers and a study of the weather patterns on the night of the murder break the case open and reveal the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Hikers near Anchorage, Alaska discovered a body wrapped in sheets which were edged in orange stitching. Clinging to the sheet, they also discovered a tuft of red carpet fibers _ threads of evidence which led them straight to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Russ Stager, a popular gym teacher, is found dead of what appears to be an accidental gunshot wound, his family becomes suspicious. They think his wife may have planned his murder. When police find that her previous husband died in questionable circumstances, they re-examine the crime scene and uncover evidence that the death was no accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Forensic scientists work with the only clue recovered from a multiple-victim shooting: a 12-gauge shotgun. Scientists use unique methods to lift the serial number from the weapon in order to trace it to its owner.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is shot to death in her store just one day before she is to testify against the man accused of robbing her. The robber becomes the prime suspect, but he has a solid alibi: a time-stamped videotape of his activities on the day of the murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1999, a 12-year-old was found murdered in her home and it changed the feeling of security residents had in the small town of Waseca, Minnesota. Despite a meticulous search of the home and an exhaustive investigation, police had no suspects.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 19-year-old woman was found dead in her car; the scene had been staged to make murder appear to be suicide. During their investigation, police discovered the victim was involved in a love quadrangle, giving several people a motive to kill her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Detectives learn that a genetic disorder caused the deaths of a woman's children, resulting in an overturned conviction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A fifteen-year-old boy died from a mysterious gunshot wound to his head while he was sitting in the lobby of his father's gun club. Ballistics, laser technology, made-to-scale models and the latest in forensic animation showed that bullet had followed a tragic course after a misfire at the outdoor range.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A beautiful young woman fell to her death from a cliff overlooking the ocean. Initially, investigators thought it was a tragic accident. But after a forensic examination, police had a very different picture of what happened.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1999, nine-year-old Valiree Jackson vanished on her way to school near Spokane, WA. Her father Brad Jackson reported the disappearance and the entire community started searching for her abductor.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a wealthy real estate tycoon went missing, it appeared to be foul play. In his will, he left instructions regarding what was to happen if he died under violent circumstances_which were carried out after a hiker came across a bullet-ridden skull.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The police investigate when a severed leg is discovered in the garbage. Conventional means of identifying the victim are impossible, leading investigators to rely on DNA, forensic anthropology and toxicology to identify the victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1992, 22-year-old Dawn Bruce is brutally murdered in her Virginia apartment. Investigators notice a blood smear on a pillow case that appears to have been made by one of the killer's fingers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is killed while walking her dogs in the woods. Since she had no enemies and her husband has an alibi, police assume it was a hunting accident...until a strange letter turns up allegedly written by the woman before her death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The disappearance of Philadelphia college athlete Aimee Willard is investigated after her car was found on the roadside. When her body is found, the police find unusual marks on her body and DNA evidence that eventually lead to her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1980, Michigan resident Shannon Mohr died in what was reported to be a horseback riding accident by her new husband David Davis. After her death, Shannon's family reported to police their suspicions that Davis seemed far from grief-stricken.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police suspect Dr. John 'Jack' Boyle in the disappearance of his wife Noreen when he signs mortgage papers for a new house with a woman pretending to be his spouse.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young TV news producer is raped and murdered in her apartment. Police identify two suspects, but both are cleared of any wrongdoing. The case stalls for more than a year, and then investigators turn to the Commonwealth of Virginia's DNA Databank.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murder of 9-year-old Jessica Knott is investigated. Investigators use a garbage bag to connect suspect James Edward Crow, Jr. to the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man is found dead in his home, and his ex-wife has a perfect alibi. To determine time of death, investigators need to know when the victim ate his last meal. An endocrinologist, a forensic botanist, and a short-order cook answer their question.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the decomposed body of a teenage girl is discovered in Pennsylvania, police have no clues to her identity. But weeks earlier, stabbing victim Kathy Sagusti told investigators she thought she might have heard a murder taking place in her basement.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In this classic episode of Forensic Files, the longest running true crime series in television history, a serial bomber was waging a vendetta against the legal profession: A judge, two attorneys and even a courthouse had been targeted. Employing a range of forensic techniques, investigators used evidence from the bombs to lead them to the perpetrator.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Krista Harrison is the 11-year-old murder victim of Robert Anthony Buell. There are few clues to the killer's identity, but authorities are intrigued by unusual orange fibers embedded in clothing found near the victim. Months go by and the case goes cold, until a van turns up with what appear to be the same orange fibers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A suspect's former lover comes forward with a fantastic tale of murder, mutilation and cremation, but there's no way to test the story's validity until a plant pathologist and a dendrochronology's conduct tests where the cremation supposedly occurred

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Exploring how an American hit single from 1966 -- "The Cheater" by Bob Kuban and the In-Men -- foreshadowed the demise of the group's lead singer, Walter Scott.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.desc: Police learn that a pregnant 16-year-old vanished from school and suspect that she ran away with her baby�s father. Detectives race to find her, they uncover a killer hiding in plain sight and a horrific secret that will change her family forever.

programme.desc: Quinn and Vince get a lead on Nacole's case and learn about another cold case from 2005. Solving these cases proves to be a test of patience, but as retirement draws near, the detectives begin to wonder: will Nacole's case ever be solved?

programme.desc: Quinn and Velazquez uncover the secret life of a young mother when she is found murdered, the victim of a psychopathic killer preying on high class prostiitutes.

programme.desc: In the early morning of New Year�s Day 2009, Oakland resident Oscar Grant is shot in the back by a BART transit officer at Fruitvale Station. Within days, dramatic cell phone videos of Oscar�s shooting go public, igniting a global fight for justice.

programme.desc: After a hail of gunfire leaves two people dead, police quickly identify a suspect, but they struggle to pin him for the crime until a strange coincidence makes them realize he’s already in their crosshairs for another murder.

programme.desc: The village of Midsomer Mallow is one of the villages hoping to win the title of the "Perfect English Village" competition. As the story unfolds, not everything is "perfect" as local thief Peter Drinkwater is stabbed with a pitchfork at an abandoned farm, he's using as a squat. Shortly after robbing a retired actor's home. Despite the villagers' best efforts to keep the murder quiet so as to not ruin their chances in the competition, several further deaths occur eventually bringing Detective Chief Inspector Barnaby back several decades to uncover the killer.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Cully's latest boyfriend, Nico, decides to shadow Troy in preparation for a TV role he recently got, but gets more hands-on experience than he expected when investigations into a slashed painting at a museum in Aspern Tallow, a 17th century portrait of Royalist Jonathan Lowrie, are overshadowed by the murder of his descendant, Marcus Lowrie. It isn't long before Barnaby and Troy, with Nico's assistance, find themselves dealing with mysterious, ghostly events, escaped criminals, and a museum trustee who has more to them than meets the eye.

programme.title: LEADERS with Francine Lacqua:Alexander Stubb, President of Finland
programme.desc: What drives the world’s most influential minds? In Leaders with Francine Lacqua, Bloomberg’s award-winning anchor sits down with the decision-makers shaping our future to explore the personal stories behind their public personas. Discover what inspires them, how they navigate power and the lessons they offer on defining success. programme.title: Torrente sangriento
programme.desc: En un penal de máxima seguridad se dan a la fuga los hombres más peligrosos y, con tal de no ser capturados, dejan a su paso una terrible masacre.

programme.title: gente de accionar
programme.desc: Renato y su amigo son dos jóvenes traficantes decididos a tomar el control de la ciudad utilizando su valor y su visión para los negocios.

programme.title: El Tacuache 2.0
programme.desc: El Tacuache heredó las riendas del cartel y ahora es el patrón. Tendrá que lidiar con divertidas situaciones para mantener el mando junto con su equipo de seguridad.

programme.title: Rata de 2 patas
programme.desc: Gerardo es un mujeriego que es capaz de hacer lo que sea con tal de conseguir lo que quiere.

programme.title: Brindis mortal
programme.desc: Ramón, hijo de un poderoso narcotraficante, anhela convertirse en la mano derecha de su padre y formar parte de su grupo de sicarios.

programme.title: Misión extrajudicial
programme.desc: Sebastián Ornelas, además de ser el amo y señor de todo el poblado, es el hombre más buscado por las autoridades. Un día, su guapa mujer conoce a un judicial, quién está a cargo de su captura, y ahí comienza la verdadera cacería.

programme.title: Chicas bien
programme.desc: En un intento de pasar un buen rato y olvidar el posible divorcio de sus padres, Fernanda decide invitar a sus mejores amigas a pasar un fin de semana en su casa de campo.

programme.title: Amar A Morir
programme.desc: Esta es la historia de Alejandro y Rosa, dos personas de diferentes clases sociales que se enamoran y arriesgan sus vidas en busca de la libertad.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Con dolor y resignación, Lucas acepta su destino con Marisa y el bebé que van a tener. Jade se entera de que Lucas va a ser padre y rompe en llanto. Cristina averigua por el papá del niño de Dora.

programme.title: Mi amor de las estrellas
programme.desc: Song-yi está en la sala de emergencias después de un accidente que Jae-kyung planeó. Min-joon piensa culparse por todas las acciones malvadas de Jae-kyung.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Sus hijos le hacen creer a Israel que una fiera se comió a José. Una batalla le da la oportunidad de huir a José, pero decide ayudar a Abumani. Todos están alerta en el palacio ante la coronación. Menkhe se siente atraído por Adja.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: María do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón, pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Sinan busca a Öyku y al ver que está con Metin, se presenta como su novio. Orhan y Busra se besan y Selim los ve. Öyku va a casa de Metin para disculparse, pero Mahinur es dura con ella y le pide alejarse.

programme.title: El Laberinto de Alicia
programme.desc: Sandra llega a la casa del Gato y Manuel los descubre. El Gato ya tiene una nueva víctima. Ramón le pide un favor a Alicia.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Jade quiere otra esposa para Said y habla con Muna, una mujer bella y soltera, le dice que Said busca otra mujer. Cristina invita a Leonardo a la fiesta de Daniel, Albieri está nervioso.

programme.title: Mi amor de las estrellas
programme.desc: Min-joon confronta a Jae-kyung sobre todas las vidas que ha destruido. Song-yi le dice a Min-joon que se aleje de ella, pero hace lo opuesto.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Abumani y José hacen un pacto. Israel ha desaparecido del campamento y no saben de qué es capaz con su dolor. Sheshi prueba su fuerza y capacidad para ser nombrado Faraón de Egipto. Un atentado contra el nuevo Faraón, falla en su cometido.

programme.title: Señora del Destino

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Think Bigfoot’s a big joke? Talk of UFOs make you glaze over? Tonight we’re talking to people who say – keep an open mind! We wander the woods with members of “Squatchachusetts” who are hot on the pursuit of information on the (could it be?) existence of Bigfoot. Anthony Everett boards the historic USS Salem in Quincy, where a paranormal investigator gives him the grand tour. And for anyone looking for evidence of UFOs or alien life, Anthony talks to a Harvard astrophysicist who researches potential evidence of alien technology.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: A trophy case under the Longfellow Bridge, and a steampunk museum inside a central Mass. church? Who knew! They’re just two of the quirky stories that Ted Reinstein uncovers tonight. Ted heads inside a local stove hospital (just what it sounds like) and the Volleyball Hall of Fame (can you guess where it is?). He meets a local man who makes baseballs…by hand…and visits a Cambridge candy shop that has a “sweet” collection of confectionery memorabilia.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: From head to toe, wool is an indispensable fiber and tonight we meet local shepherds, sheep, shearers (and sheep dogs!) as we explore the wool trade in New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: New England wouldn’t be New England without the shore birds, butterflies, and turtles that spend part of the year here. These and other local creatures are considered “indicator species” that also help us understand the impact of habitat loss and climate change. Tonight we get up close to giant sea turtles and tiny terrapins, whimbrels and piping plovers, and meet the people committed to protecting them.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Press a button and anything you want will appear. This may sound like a scene from the 1960s cartoon, The Jetsons, but this is life in 2021. Popular delivery apps report growth of more than 100% year to year, and now, local businesses are getting into the on-demand game: gourmet cupcakes, pet groomers, mechanics, and haircuts that come right to you.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Since a major offshore wind project was first proposed in Nantucket Sound more than 20 years ago New Englanders have been waiting for the big wind energy boom. Now, the first turbines are finally being installed in the sea bed. Tonight we find out the “when and where” of local wind projects, and talk to experts about why Massachusetts is considered the “Saudi Arabia” of offshore wind. We visit New Bedford, which is investing big-time in the “blue economy.” Finally, we meet some critics who fear the impact on sea life and the commercialization of the ocean.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Wooded beauty, winter quiet, and a deep sense of community - Ted Reinstein finds it all in this collection of small towns in the southeast corner of the Green Mountain State. He hits Peru, Jamaica, and Athens - just a few of the more peculiar place names in northern New England – and meets the couple who have revived a beloved general store. Ted also ventures a bit outside Windham County to hit the slopes at Bromley Mountain, a New England leader in adaptive skiing.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Since a major offshore wind project was first proposed in Nantucket Sound more than 20 years ago New Englanders have been waiting for the big wind energy boom. Now, the first turbines are finally being installed in the sea bed. Tonight we find out the “when and where” of local wind projects, and talk to experts about why Massachusetts is considered the “Saudi Arabia” of offshore wind. We visit New Bedford, which is investing big-time in the “blue economy.” Finally, we meet some critics who fear the impact on sea life and the commercialization of the ocean.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Anthony Everett visits scenic Windsor County, Vt., during “shoulder season.” We begin in Woodstock, visiting the charming Woodstock Inn and Marsh-Billings-Rockefeller National Historic Park. In nearby Bridgewater, Anthony meets furniture maker Charles Shackleton (a cousin of explorer Sir Ernest Shackleton - he of lost ship “Endurance” fame). Anthony ventures over the county line to Randolph, where he meets a Thai chef now gaining a national reputation. And back in West Windsor, home to Ascutney Mountain Resort, we visit “the town that saved itself.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: California has Napa Valley. New England has…more wine than you might think! Our region is home to 600 vineyards and countless stories of winemakers and wine innovators. Shayna Seymour boards a renovated school bus for a wine tour along the North Shore. We visit a pop-up wine bar in an old shipping container in Boston’s Fenway neighborhood; make a stop on Connecticut’s official Wine Trail, at Taylor Brooke Winery, where the business is all in the family; and meet a local artist who custom-paints wine glasses.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: The Nutcracker is back! So are big theatrical movie releases and live theater performances, after a pandemic-related hiatus. Tonight, Shayna Seymour sashays through Boston Ballet’s costume shop and meets dancers preparing for a return to live performances. Boston movie critic Ty Burr joins us for some Oscar talk, highlighting 2021 Academy Award contenders, and Nicole Estaphan sits down with Grammy-winning bassist Esperanza Spalding to discuss her work on a groundbreaking new opera, Iphigenia.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Looking for some entertainment? Tonight we bring you a taste of the musicals, movies, and arts performances on tap this holiday season and winter. Shayna Seymour meets a Berklee grad and one of the “queens” from the rock musical, “Six,” and we check on other stage performances including a new theater company, The Front Porch Arts Collective. Movie critic Ty Burr gives us a rundown of the Academy Award contenders, and Shayna Seymour goes behind the scenes of The Boston Ballet’s “The Nutcracker.”

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Bundle up! Tonight, Anthony Everett is taking us out for a cold-weather version of “On the Water.” He heads out on the chilly waters of New Hampshire’s Great Bay, where UNH researchers are studying the behavior of oysters.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Winter is coming...and while winter farming sounds like an oxymoron, growing, cultivating, and selling produce still happens during the coldest months of the year. Tonight, we visit a winter CSA selling produce year-round, a fifth-generation farm specializing in winter grain-making, and a greenhouse restaurant at Natick’s Lookout Farm. We’ll also meet a farmer from the Azores who settled in Massachusetts and is now helping other immigrant farmers.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Winter weather is still in our forecast, and we have the warm and cozy menu to match! Chicken and dumplings. Shrimp and grits. Mac and cheese. Darryl’s Corner Bar and Kitchen in Roxbury whips up Southern comfort food staples, and we cozy up to the hearth at Hunter’s Kitchen and Bar in South Boston. Erika Tarantal carbo-loads with warm, savory English muffins at Vinal Bakery in Somerville, while in Cambridge we indulge in heaps of ramen and Thai home cooking. Plus – what’s not to like about an apres-ski warm-up in… Boston?

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: “The prettiest village in Maine.” That’s how a writer once described Wiscasset, a coastal enclave in mid-coast Maine. Tonight Nicole Estaphan takes in the spectacular views and meets the people who call this historic village home. She makes a whistle stop at the local train museum; visits a lush lavender farm; and stops by a yoga retreat where she communes with the resident alpacas. And before leaving town Nicole meets a local baker whose key lime pie one food writer called the best – ever.

programme.desc: Tonight we focus on the booming world of skin care. We meet a local dermatologist who started a program focused on patients of color and their unique skin health needs; and a doctor who launched a program to screen firefighters for skin cancer.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Ever wonder who invented the golf tee or where ear muffs made their debut? Ted Reinstein is celebrating “yankee ingenuity” with a tour of homegrown innovations dreamed up by local inventors and found in countless New England barns and basements. Ted meets the owner of one of the largest collections of apple parers (yes, really) as well as the Maine inventor of a wallet for the front pocket. And no discussion of yankee ingenuity would be complete without a visit to the Vermont Country Store, where we find gadgets aplenty.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Ted Reinstein is headed to York County, home to fascinating history, a long artistic tradition, spectacular natural beauty and of course, a diner! Ted stops by a one-time ski resort turned conservation area. In Sanford, he grabs a bite at the Third Alarm Diner, finds a hit with the Sanford Mainers baseball team, and catches up with a theater company in Saco. In Alfred, Ted explores the history of the area’s once-thriving Shaker community. And he wraps it all by sampling one of Maine’s famous Holy Donuts.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight, we’re eating food with lots of sabor, the Spanish word for flavor. First on the menu, chicharrones and other Cuban classics served up at Gustazo in Waltham; mofongo and pollo frito at Merengue in Boston (a David Ortiz favorite); Anthony Everett samples a Spanish delicacy-- roasted bone marrow with oxtail marmalade-- at Toro in the South End; and finally, we discover the hidden door to Casa Romero where diners have enjoyed the chile relleno dish for nearly 50 years.
